message:
feat(lab): evidence normalization for lab-vs-live comparison (2a6k9.4.3)

Adds concrete normalization paths from both execution environments:

- normalize_lab_report(): maps LabRunReport into NormalizedSemantics
- normalize_lab_observable(): builds NormalizedObservable with provenance
- normalize_live_observable(): wraps LiveRunResult into NormalizedObservable

6 new tests (104 total dual_run tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2531,7 +2531,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","title":"Capture live-runtime semantic evidence and normalize it for comparison","description":"Build the live-side adapter, instrumentation, and normalization pipeline needed to compare real executions against lab expectations without depending on fragile raw event-order equality, and verify that translation layer directly with deterministic fixtures and mismatch diagnostics.","design":"This phase makes live execution observable enough to compare meaningfully. The requirement is not perfect raw trace identity; it is sufficient semantic evidence to answer the comparator questions defined in the contract phase.

Live-side collection should prefer stable semantic markers over brittle internal details. The runtime adapter should aim for execution paths that reduce gratuitous scheduler noise, such as current-thread setups where appropriate, while still exercising the actual live code paths under test.

Normalization is a first-class design concern here. The output should collapse lab and live evidence into the same semantic record type so the differential engine is comparing comparable facts instead of raw heterogeneous traces.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a live-runtime adapter that can execute differential scenarios and emit semantic evidence suitable for comparison.

Normalization rules exist for the main observables under test and avoid depending on unstable raw ordering where ordering is not part of the contract.

The live capture and normalization path has dedicated unit/fixture coverage, representative golden records, and field-level mismatch diagnostics rather than being tested only transitively through later pilots.","notes":"This phase should reuse test setup and runtime-builder patterns already present in `tests/common/mod.rs` and related runtime tests. It is intentionally separate from the comparator phase because evidence collection and mismatch judgment are different responsibilities.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000549348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","epic","evidence","live","trace","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000491300Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:11.051481361Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:14.016051971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","title":"Build current-thread live runner adapter for differential scenarios","description":"Create the live execution adapter that runs shared scenarios through real runtime code paths while minimizing gratuitous scheduling noise and preserving reproducibility.","design":"Build the live-side execution adapter that can take a shared scenario and drive the actual runtime paths under test with as little incidental nondeterminism as practical. Prefer current-thread or otherwise constrained runtime setups where that reduces scheduler noise without changing the semantics the surface is supposed to exercise.

The adapter should be explicit about what it controls and what it cannot control. It must not quietly turn live execution into another simulated path. The whole point is to run the real code paths while still making comparison practical.","acceptance_criteria":"A live-run adapter exists and can execute shared differential scenarios through real runtime code paths using a documented execution model rather than ad hoc per-suite setup.

Adapter bootstrapping, run lifecycle, and teardown behavior have direct unit or contract coverage plus at least one smoke-level e2e path using the same live-run surface the later pilots and scripts will use.

The adapter emits structured run metadata rich enough for later reports and replay guidance, including scenario identity, profile, seed lineage, runtime mode, and any known nondeterminism qualifiers.","notes":"This task is about controlling noise, not about hiding the fact that the live path is live.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562404216Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562379841Z","close_reason":"Implemented LiveRunnerAdapter: LiveRunnerConfig, LiveExecutionProfile, LiveWitnessCollector, LiveRunMetadata, LiveRunResult, run_live_adapter(). 10 new tests (total 80). Current-thread adapter with explicit witness collection, structured metadata logging, nondeterminism qualifiers, and full integration with DualRunHarness.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","live","runner","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:46.467178655Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:49.469850852Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:52.401530918Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","title":"Add live-side semantic capture hooks for comparison-relevant events","description":"Expose the semantic facts from live executions that the comparator needs, focusing on stable hooks rather than brittle raw traces or text logs.","design":"Instrument the live runtime just enough to recover the semantic facts defined by the comparator contract. That means capturing events and counters relevant to outcomes, cancellation lifecycle, loser drain, region quiescence, obligation settlement, and any surface-specific invariants chosen for early pilots.

Instrumentation should prefer stable semantic hooks over brittle log scraping or raw trace deltas. If a surface cannot be observed well enough yet, that should become a first-class limitation rather than an excuse to compare on shaky proxies.","acceptance_criteria":"The live execution path exposes the semantic evidence required by the normalized comparator schema through stable semantic hooks rather than fragile textual logs or incidental implementation details.

Missing observability is explicit and machine-readable rather than silently approximated, and capture-field behavior is covered by direct unit or fixture checks instead of being trusted only transitively.

The emitted evidence payloads and logs are structured enough that later normalization, reports, and crashpacks can trust the field names, missingness semantics, and provenance of each captured fact.","notes":"Better instrumentation beats more tolerant comparison logic. This task should bias toward improving evidence quality.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:50.817780901Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:50.817756225Z","close_reason":"Implemented semantic capture hooks: CaptureManifest with observed/inferred/unsupported tracking, capture_terminal_outcome() from Outcome<T,E>, capture_terminal_from_result(), capture_obligation_balance(), capture_region_close(), capture_loser_drain(), capture_cancellation(). 18 new tests (total 98). Explicit FieldObservability annotations make missing observability machine-readable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","instrumentation","live","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:58.499190711Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:55.386840494Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","title":"Normalize lab and live evidence into shared comparator records","description":"Build the actual normalization path that converts heterogeneous lab/live evidence into the same semantic record type for comparison.","design":"Implement the mapping that takes lab evidence and live evidence and collapses both into the canonical normalized record defined earlier. This includes canonical ordering for data that is semantically unordered, consistent naming for obligations and regions, and explicit elision of incidental detail.

Where the mapping is lossy, the loss must be intentional and justified. The project should be able to explain exactly why omitted detail is not needed to judge semantic parity for the chosen surface.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a concrete normalization path from both execution environments into the shared comparator record, and unit or fixture coverage exercises representative positive and negative mappings directly.

Canonical example mappings and golden record shapes are pinned, and field-level diff output plus structured diagnostics make false-positive and false-negative risk reviewable when normalization behavior changes.

At least one reusable downstream consumer beyond the local normalization tests uses the same normalized shape end-to-end, so later runner and reporting flows are not quietly relying on a different record contract.","notes":"This task is where semantic comparison becomes real instead of aspirational.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:07:49.279820374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","observability","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:04.471910998Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:01.449827579Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

This includes canonical ordering for data that is semantically unordered, consistent naming for obligations and regions, and explicit elision of incidental detail.

Where the mapping is lossy, the loss must be intentional and justified. The project should be able to explain exactly why omitted detail is not needed to judge semantic parity for the chosen surface.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a concrete normalization path from both execution environments into the shared comparator record, and unit or fixture coverage exercises representative positive and negative mappings directly.

Canonical example mappings and golden record shapes are pinned, and field-level diff output plus structured diagnostics make false-positive and false-negative risk reviewable when normalization behavior changes.

At least one reusable downstream consumer beyond the local normalization tests uses the same normalized shape end-to-end, so later runner and reporting flows are not quietly relying on a different record contract.","notes":"This task is where semantic comparison becomes real instead of aspirational.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:40:46.639817706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","observability","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:04.471910998Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:01.449827579Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","title":"Define time and scheduler-noise normalization policy for live re-execution","description":"Specify how virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise should be treated when comparing lab and live results so the framework stays semantically sharp without becoming brittle.","design":"Define how the framework reconciles virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise during live re-execution. Some surfaces will require ordering or timing windows to be normalized; others must reject timing-sensitive comparison entirely until the effect boundary is better controlled.

This task should set the explicit policy for when time-based facts are comparable, when they are only advisory, and when they are out of scope. It should also say how reruns interact with timing noise so the comparator does not accidentally ratchet itself into useless tolerance.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a written normalization policy for time-related and scheduler-noise-related evidence.

The policy makes it clear which time facts are part of semantic comparison and which are not.

The policy also defines how suppression, qualification, or explanation of scheduler noise appears in reports and verdict semantics so later CI and operator flows do not invent their own tolerance language.","notes":"Claimed after bv --robot-plan ranked the differential track as the clean unowned unblocker lane; implementing time and scheduler-noise normalization policy with contract coverage and downstream bindings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteCrane","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:17.493967068Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:39:17.493947782Z","close_reason":"Completed time and scheduler-noise normalization policy, contract test, and downstream differential bindings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","time","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:07.420718160Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:10.395240991Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:13.352500745Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","title":"Build live evidence capture and normalization unit suite with golden records","description":"Test the live-capture and normalization pipeline directly with deterministic fixtures, negative cases, and field-level mismatch diagnostics.","design":"Add the deterministic verification suite for live-side capture and normalization. This should cover positive and negative cases for evidence extraction, missing-observability behavior, canonical ordering, lossy normalization decisions, and representative golden records that prove lab and live evidence collapse into the intended shared shape.

The suite should also require detailed diff logging when normalization mismatches occur. If a normalization regression happens, the output should show exactly which semantic field diverged and which source evidence produced it.","acceptance_criteria":"Live evidence capture and normalization have dedicated unit/fixture coverage rather than being tested only transitively through later pilots.

Representative golden lab/live records are checked into the verification story and are easy to diff when behavior changes.

Normalization failures emit detailed field-level diagnostics that make root-cause analysis fast, and at least one targeted end-to-end path proves the same capture/normalization surface behaves correctly when exercised through the real runner flow.","notes":"Normalization is too central to leave under-tested. This bead makes it a first-class verification target instead of a hidden implementation detail.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:49.979386588Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:52:36.209905637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","golden","normalization","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:02.945870384Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:52:36.209849853Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:18:49.979386588Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:05.894942486Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:08.837063353Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:27:11.799783389Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","title":"Implement differential execution, divergence classification, and repro artifacts","description":"Assemble the execution engine that runs the same scenario through lab and live paths, classifies mismatches, emits minimized repro bundles when parity fails, and exposes reusable end-to-end runner/report entrypoints that make the system practical for humans and CI.","design":"This phase assembles the actual differential judge. Its job is to run both paths, compare normalized records, and preserve evidence aggressively when parity fails.

The design should treat repro quality as part of correctness. A differential test that only says "mismatch" without a seed, witness, or replay bundle creates more frustration than confidence. The system should therefore integrate minimization, classification, and artifact generation tightly rather than bolt them on after the fact.

This phase should also guard against false positives by distinguishing unsupported surfaces, missing observability, and scheduler-only noise from true semantic divergence.","acceptance_criteria":"A reusable differential runner exists and can execute the same scenario in lab and live paths.

Divergences are classified and emitted with enough artifact material to reproduce or minimize them.

Reusable e2e runner scripts exist and produce detailed structured logs plus a concise operator-facing summary/report.

The framework includes negative-control calibration coverage proving that intentional mismatches are detected, classified, and reported correctly rather than only passing on positive-parity cases.","notes":"Existing inspiration includes the differential-harness shape in `tests/raptorq_conformance.rs` plus replay/debugging workflows already documented in `docs/replay-debugging.md`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:08.972914180Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:47:39.050838654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["comparator","differential","epic","replay","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:08.972914180Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:17.103173971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:20.073452489Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:23.019244933Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2oh2u.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:28.895500575Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3cddg.12.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:25.965022831Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -4730,7 +4730,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.17","title":"[FABRIC][P15.17] Reactive protocol synthesis","description":"Synthesize correct scaffolding from protocol contracts. File: src/messaging/session/synthesis.rs

- From protocol contract, produce: handler skeletons for each role, obligation registration/completion at correct protocol points, error/timeout branches, compensation hooks for saga workflows, adapters for compatible protocol evolution
- Developer supplies business logic; fabric supplies conversation skeleton, obligation wiring, compatibility checks
- Moves distributed plumbing bugs out of artisanal handler code
- Does NOT claim to produce correct business logic (only correct protocol scaffolding)
- Tests: synthesize handler for simple request/reply, streaming protocol, saga, verify obligation wiring, test evolved protocol adapter","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:31.681346054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.376345993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","synthesis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.17","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:31.681346054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.17","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.376272827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","title":"[FABRIC][P15.18] Speculative subject-cell execution","description":"Tentative publish with deterministic rollback for low-conflict cells. File: src/messaging/fabric.rs (speculative portion)

- For low-conflict subject cells and service classes that permit it:
  1. Origin issues tentative publish backed by tentative obligation
  2. Control capsule sequences in background
  3. If confirmed: obligation commits, tentative path becomes final
  4. If conflict: tentative obligation aborts deterministically, corrected outcome surfaced (no illegal intermediate state exposed to consumers)
- Bounded by: service class, cell-local conflict histograms, explicit kill switches
- Verified under same replay/oracle machinery as cancellation and redelivery races
- NOT generic 'superposed execution' — narrowly justified latency optimization with explicit rollback
- Tests: successful speculative publish (confirm), conflict rollback (abort), kill switch, conflict histogram tracking, replay verification","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:46.636781540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56.864886486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","speculative"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:46.636781540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2538,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Speculative execution guardrails: (1) Bounded by service class, (2) Bounded by cell-local conflict histograms — auto-disable above threshold, (3) Bounded by explicit operator kill switches, (4) Verified under same replay/oracle machinery, (5) Tentative obligations are distinct type, (6) Consumers NEVER see tentative results, (7) Rollback is deterministic. NOT generic superposed execution — narrowly justified latency optimization.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:10:54Z"},{"id":2541,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Speculative execution guardrails (CRITICAL — must be enforced): This feature is NOT generic 'superposed execution.' It is a narrowly justified latency optimization with EXPLICIT rollback semantics. Guardrails: (1) Bounded by service class — only permitted for classes that explicitly allow it. (2) Bounded by cell-local conflict histograms — if conflict rate exceeds threshold, speculation is disabled for that cell. (3) Bounded by explicit kill switches — operator can disable per-class or per-cell. (4) Verified under the SAME replay and oracle machinery as cancellation and redelivery races — speculation must not introduce behaviors that escape the existing verification framework. (5) Tentative obligations are a distinct obligation type that cannot be confused with committed obligations. (6) Consumer-visible state NEVER includes tentative results — consumers see only committed state. (7) Rollback is deterministic — same conflict always produces the same rollback outcome. Do NOT claim this is universally beneficial. It is a targeted optimization for specific workload patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.19","title":"[FABRIC][P15.19] Subject-native multi-tenant namespace kernel","description":"NATS accounts/imports/exports already look like an OS namespace model. Asupersync should lean into this analogy. Subjects become:
- **Process mailboxes**: each actor/service addressable via subject
- **Service names**: service discovery via subject registration
- **Control channels**: per-service and per-tenant control subjects
- **Durable stream capture rules**: stream capture policy tied to subject namespace
- **Trust boundaries**: import/export morphisms define security perimeters
- **Observability feeds**: per-tenant and per-service telemetry subjects
The runtime starts to look like a "secure, replayable, distributed namespace kernel."
This is NOT about pretending to solve full CPU/memory/storage isolation — it is the namespace analogy applied to subject-based addressing. The capability model (Cx + tokens) provides the authority enforcement. The morphism system provides the namespace mapping.
- File: integrated across src/messaging/subject.rs, capability/, and control.rs
- Tests: multi-tenant namespace setup, cross-tenant isolation, namespace-as-service-discovery, namespace-as-trust-boundary","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","multi-tenant","namespace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.19","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.20","title":"[FABRIC][P15.20] Deterministic incident rehearsal","description":"Because Asupersync-hosted streams, consumers, gateway handlers, and advisories live inside replayable runtime semantics, operators can do:
- "Replay this outage with a different consumer policy"
- "Replay this partition with a stricter export policy"
- "Replay this drain event with a shorter budget"
- "Replay this leaf reconnect storm against a candidate scheduler"
This is much closer to "chaos engineering with proof obligations" than ordinary messaging infrastructure. Unlike conventional simulation, the branch starts from semantically meaningful state rather than an approximate test fixture.
Distinct from P15.4 (Continuous Counterfactual Control): incident rehearsal is post-hoc analysis of specific past events; counterfactual control is ongoing policy evaluation. They share the cut/branch infrastructure but have different operational workflows.
- Requires: forkable snapshots (P5.4), cut-certified mobility (P15.2), lab runtime integration
- File: src/messaging/cut.rs (rehearsal portion)
- Tests: capture outage state, fork into lab runtime, replay with different policy, compare outcomes, verify deterministic replay","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:44:04.775561811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T16:45:20.276235795Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","incident","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.20","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:44:04.775561811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.20","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:45:20.276167117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.3","title":"[FABRIC][P15.3] Subject-native workflow and saga kernel","description":"Build workflow/saga primitives on the subject fabric. File: src/messaging/service.rs (workflow portion)

- Durable workflow step = subject transition + obligations (reply, lease, timeout, compensation, deadline)
- Unresolved work is NOT inferred from counters — represented directly in obligation ledger
- One substrate for: request/reply, retries, outbox, sagas, and compensation
- Replayable partial progress with precise 'what is still owed?'
- Recovery inherits same no-orphan and no-silent-drop rules as runtime
- WorkflowStep struct: step_id, subject, obligations, compensation_path, timeout, status
- SagaState struct: saga_id, steps, current_step, compensated_steps, evidence_trail
- Tests: linear workflow execution, compensation on failure, partial progress tracking, recovery after crash, 'what is still owed' query","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:30:18.918197424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:30:18.918197424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","saga","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:30:18.918197424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.4","title":"[FABRIC][P15.4] Continuous counterfactual control","description":"Build shadow branch capability for continuous policy evaluation. File: src/messaging/cut.rs (branch portion)

- From any cut-certified snapshot: fork multiple candidate worlds
- Replay same in-boundary history under different: retry laws, morphisms, placement rules, coding budgets, supervision policies
- Decision layer compares branches and promotes only policies that beat current within explicit safety envelopes
- Operations become always-on policy laboratory
- Adaptive control becomes evidence-backed promotion (not heuristic toggles)
- Incidents become reusable evaluation data
- High-stakes changes become branch/score/promote workflows
- Branch starts from semantically meaningful state (not approximate test fixture)
- Tests: fork from cut, replay with different policy, compare branch outcomes, promote winning policy, safety envelope enforcement","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:30:29.510910867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:56.890964973Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","counterfactual","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:30:29.510910867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:56.890896155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
